step.title: Generate dev-env seed-admin goal (conditional, phase=seed)
step.rule: Runs strictly AFTER step 3.19 so every phase=auth and phase=auth-bootstrap goal ID already exists in goals.yaml.
CONDITIONAL — emit at most ONE goal, and only when seeding was requested AND at least one auth flow exists. NEVER fold this into the test-only fixtures goal (phase=fixtures, --env=test).

Credentials are referenced by env-var name only (admin_identifier_env, admin_password_env) — NEVER inline a secret value in description (GM-05). Seeds the dev environment ONLY — NEVER prod, NEVER --env=test.
All inputs read from docs/architecture/* files on disk (G-16), never from conversation context.

substep.title: Read seed decision and gate
substep.action: Read docs/architecture/test-environment.md. Locate the `## Bootstrap / Seed Default Admin` block. Extract seed_default_admin and the env-var NAMES admin_identifier_env, admin_password_env.
Read docs/architecture/cross-cutting.md auth flows (the SAME source used by step 3.19.1). Count N_auth_flows.

branch.condition: seed_default_admin != yes OR the Bootstrap / Seed Default Admin block is absent
branch.action: Print: "Seed default admin not requested — skipping Step 3.19a."
SKIP — proceed to next step. Do NOT call goal-create.
Print WARNING: "Seed default admin was requested but no auth flows exist — there is no User entity to seed. Skipping Step 3.19a."
SKIP — proceed to next step. Do NOT call goal-create.

substep.title: Resolve depends_on
substep.action: Read goals.yaml from disk. Find the goal with phase=auth-bootstrap — store its ID as SEED_DEP. The auth-bootstrap goal (step 3.16a) owns the User entity + config/packages/security.yaml, so the seed must run after it.
FALLBACK: if no phase=auth-bootstrap goal exists (D3 not present in this plan), resolve the first phase=auth goal (Register — owner of the User entity per step 3.19) and use its ID as SEED_DEP.

check.condition: neither a phase=auth-bootstrap goal nor any phase=auth goal resolves
check.action: ABORT with error: "Cannot generate seed-admin goal: no auth-bootstrap or auth goal found in goals.yaml — the seed depends on the User entity owner."

substep.rule: SEED_DEP MUST be dynamically resolved from goals.yaml — never hardcoded (G-03).

substep.title: Derive the coarse deliverable_area
substep.action: Set SEED_AREA = the seed-admin command deliverable directory in the resolved topology — `projects/{app}/src/Command/` (the deployable-app command layer where the `app:seed-admin` console command class lives), where {app} is the deployable name from the resolved conventions/topology. This is a COARSE path (the depinfer produce/consume seed + the elaborator's tree-read anchor), NOT a glob and NOT a per-file path. Keep the `{app}` placeholder as the shard already uses it.
substep.rule: Do NOT resolve the seed command name from templates, or author any acceptance/validate/investigator/context/not-in-scope here — those are Tier-2 (/tmux:elaborate authors them at dispatch). The roadmap skeleton commits only to the coarse deliverable_area.

substep.title: Create the dev-env seed-admin goal via goal-create (roadmap skeleton)
substep.action: Call the goal-create MCP tool with a roadmap SKELETON:

params.param: Seed default admin in dev via bin/console app:seed-admin

substep.rule: phase=seed on the created goal (a new phase value, distinct from fixtures).
Description is 56 chars — within the 120-char limit (MaxGoalDescriptionLength).

NO validate/acceptance/scope/context/investigation_config/not_in_scope params — this is a roadmap skeleton; /tmux:elaborate authors every concrete field at dispatch (design §5/§6). NEVER run `tmux-cli rules match` here.

check.action: Print the error with full context and ABORT — do not leave a partial seed goal.
Print: "Seed-admin skeleton created: goal-{NNN} (phase=seed, status=roadmap, depends_on=[{SEED_DEP}], deliverable_area={SEED_AREA}, max_retries=2)"
